Patch Set 2:

> BUILD

Peter, in this case it would have been better to rebase the patch rather than just rebuild it, since this patch is based on a parent that is 48 patches old already (Oleg has landed 2 groups of patches to master since this was pushed).

However, since there is no patch conflict and the patch has already been testing for a few hours, we may as well let it continue as-is since the test system is overloaded.

commit.subject: LU-19043 mount: check vm.dirty_ratio at client mount time
commit.message:
LU-19043 mount: check vm.dirty_ratio at client mount time

Lustre's writeback support does not work well with vm_dirty_ratio
set to 0. Values below 10 can cause inefficient write behavior
under load.

This patch adds a check during client mount that verifies the
vm.dirty_ratio sysctl setting is configured to a value of 10 or
greater. If the value is found to be too low, a warning is
displayed suggesting how to fix the issue.

This is a temporary solution while the underlying issue is being
addressed in LU-19014.

Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I82169869c40232c9068dd758dd656646e39a616c
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/59387
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Frederick Dilger <fdilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Qian Yingjin <qian@ddn.com>
